options.content:
## CI-CD / Jenkins Controller — architecture & how to read this board

This board is shaped to the **actual CI architecture** of this platform, not a generic Jenkins:

**The pipeline topology**
- A **seed job** (`seed-jobs`, Job DSL in `jenkins/pipelines/seed/`) generates one **`pipelineJob`** per service × environment: `gateway`, `gateway-develop`, `jhipstersamplemicroservice(-develop)`, `microservices-k6-smoke(-develop)`. **There is NO multibranch / organization-folder / webhook scanning.**
- Each generated job is a one-liner that calls the **declarative shared-library** pipeline `MicroservicesPipeline(...)` (`vars/MicroservicesPipeline.groovy`) — stages: Checkout → Semgrep → CodeQL → Trivy → **Build & Test** → Build & Push image → Deploy (ArgoCD GitOps) → **Smoke Test** → k6.
- Builds run on **ephemeral Kubernetes pod agents** (kubernetes-plugin, one 9-container pod per build, `containerCap` 10–20). The controller's built-in node has **`numExecutors: 0`** — it never runs builds.
- Deploy is **GitOps**: the pipeline bumps the image tag in the gitops repo and **ArgoCD** syncs it; there is no `kubectl apply` from Jenkins.

**Metric families (Jenkins OpenTelemetry plugin, service `jenkins`)**
- **`ci_pipeline_run_*`** — OTel CI/CD semantic-convention run metrics: launched / started / active / completed / **success / failed / aborted** + run **duration** histogram. ⚠️ **In this plugin version they are AGGREGATE across all pipelines** (no usable `ci_pipeline_id` breakdown — it collapses to `#other#`). So the pipeline panels show **controller-wide totals**; to inspect an **individual build / stage / step**, use the **Traces** panel (Tempo: pipeline → stage → step spans) at the bottom.
- **`jenkins_*`** — controller internals: **executors**, **build queue**, **ephemeral (cloud) agents**, **plugins**.

**Why some panels read `0` — and why that's correct here**
- 🟢 **Healthy `0`** — *Builds failed/aborted*, *Agent launch failures*, *Queue* are `0` on a green, idle controller (`…_failed_total` only exists after the first failure).
- ⚙️ **Ephemeral agents** — *Built-in executors* is the controller's static count (builds DON'T use it); **busy executors live on per-build agent pods**, so `busy > built-in` during a build is normal (the OTel plugin even double-labels each agent by node-name + pod-name). **Capacity = `containerCap`, not executors** — adding executors won't speed up builds. The real concurrency signal is **Active pipeline runs** + **Cloud agents**.
- 📈 **Needs a recent build** — `ci_pipeline_run_*` rate/duration panels read `0`/flat when no build is in the time window. Run a build or widen the range.
- ❌ **Removed: SCM-event / multibranch panels** — `jenkins_scm_event_*` track multibranch/organization-folder SCM API scanning, which **this seed-job architecture never does** (always `0`). They've been replaced by the **Ephemeral build agents** section, which is what actually reflects this setup.

panels.title: Build success rate
panels.description: Aggregate across all seed jobs, over the selected range.

panels.title: Agents online
panels.description: Connected agents (built-in node + any live ephemeral agent pods).

panels.title: Built-in executors
panels.description: Static executors on the controller's built-in node (numExecutors:0 means builds run on ephemeral agents, NOT here).

panels.title: Busy executors (all nodes)
panels.description: Busy executors across built-in + ephemeral agents. Can exceed 'Built-in executors' during a build (agents add executors; the OTel plugin also double-labels each agent). Use 'Active runs' for true concurrency.

panels.title: Queue size
panels.description: Jobs waiting for a free agent-pod slot (containerCap).

panels.title: Cloud agent pods (total)
panels.description: Ephemeral k8s agent pods provisioned+completed since controller start — ≈ total builds run.

panels.title: Pipeline runs — aggregate (all seed jobs · ci_pipeline_run_*; per-build detail in Traces below)
Run duration percentiles (p50 / p95 / p99)
panels.description: Whole-pipeline run time (aggregate). Reads 0 when no build is in the window. For per-stage timing use the Traces panel.

panels.title: Completed by result /s
panels.description: Completed runs grouped by result label on the duration metric.

panels.title: Ephemeral build agents (Kubernetes) & queue — this architecture's capacity model
Cloud agent pods completed /s
panels.description: Rate of ephemeral k8s agent pods finishing a build — the real build-throughput signal here.

panels.title: Avg queue wait time
panels.description: Mean time a job waited for a free agent-pod slot before starting.

panels.title: Controller JVM (summary — deep dive in 'CI-CD JVM internals')
Build traces — pipeline / stage / step spans (the per-job view)
panels.description: Since the run metrics above are aggregate, THIS is where you inspect an individual build: each trace = one pipeline run, with stage and step spans (Checkout, Semgrep, Build & Test, Deploy, Smoke Test…). Click a Trace ID → trace view (links to logs).

panels.title: Jenkins controller logs
panels.description: Controller pod logs. Filter by $level. (Build-agent logs are on the ephemeral pods — see a build's console / its trace.)

targets.expr: {k8s_namespace_name="jenkins"} | detected_level=~"$level" | json | line_format "{{ if .message }}{{ .message }}{{ else if .msg }}{{ .msg }}{{ else }}{{ __line__ }}{{ end }}"
